channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Young Australians reject budget as One Nation popularity climbs further
item.description: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they'd rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.
item.dc:creator: Sara Tomevska

media:group.media:description: The Redbridge poll found Pauline Hanson was the preferred prime minister among gen X voters.

item.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize castle
item.description: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

media:group.media:description: The Israeli capture of the castle and strategic ridge is a major advance against Iran-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: Horses bolt through Rome after police officer sets off fireworks
item.description: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

media:group.media:description: A horse spooked by a police officer's unauthorised fireworks bolts through Rome.

item.title: Applications to protect Victoria Park rejected ahead of construction of Olympic stadium
item.description: The federal government has rejected two applications for protection of Brisbane's Victoria Park ahead of construction on the Olympic stadium beginning tomorrow, but is still considering another five.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Loram and Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: Supporters of Victoria Park gathered to oppose the construction of the stadium on Sunday.

item.title: Divers at Laos cave search for two men thought to be trapped further down
item.description: The remaining four men from a group of five were freed from the flooded cave on Saturday evening, in extraordinary and emotional scenes, as search efforts continue for two people thought to still be trapped inside.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of the cave.

item.title: Why tonight's full moon will be a rare occurrence
item.description: If you look up to the full moon at sunset this evening, you may notice it looks ever so slightly smaller than usual.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Tonight's full moon will be a rare occurrence, but won't look all that different.

item.title: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues
item.description: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Harley Reid and West Coast proved far too strong for an undermanned Essendon side that lost another player to injury.

item.title: Australia scraps new Virginia-class submarines in AUKUS shake-up
item.description: The US, UK and Australia have announced they will "streamline" the purchase, with Australia no longer buying new submarines, and acquiring three second-hand ones.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Australia will now buy second-hand Virginia-class submarines.

item.title: Police 'open' to learning from volunteers who found remains in renewed search
item.description: A renewed search by volunteers for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby led to the discovery of remains on Saturday. It's the second time this year a group of volunteers has made a significant find during the search for a missing person.
item.dc:creator: Scout Wallen

media:group.media:description: Volunteer searchers found remains during a renewed search for missing man Peter Willoughby on Saturday.

item.title: Iran's internet revival unearths videos regime 'didn't want world to witness'
item.description: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.
item.dc:creator: Tavleen Singh

media:group.media:description: Iran’s new-found internet access unleashes anti-regime videos from protests held in January this year

item.title: Barnaby Joyce's link to how e-bikes came to be everywhere
item.description: When Barnaby Joyce was deputy prime minister and transport minister in 2021, his assistant minister Kevin Hogan made a quiet, but hugely significant change to the Road Vehicle Standards Act.
item.dc:creator: Ben Knight

media:group.media:description: There has been an explosion of e-bikes on Australian roads in recent years.

item.title: Another US air strike in the Pacific kills 'narco-terrorists' taking death toll to 202
item.description: It is the latest strike in a months-long series of attacks from the US on suspected drug trafficking boats in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.

media:group.media:description: Footage released on social platform X by the US Southern Command purports to show strikes on alleged drug-trafficking vessels in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Panthers hang on as Alamoti makes amends in remarkable Warriors clash
item.description: Penrith wing Paul Alamoti makes up for a horror defensive error with a spectacular winning try as the Panthers down the Warriors 20-18 in an exhilarating top-of-the-table clash.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Paul Alamoti scored two tries to help the Panthers to a brilliant victory in the game of the year so far.

item.title: Team 'astonished' as rider kicked out of Giro d'Italia Women
item.description: Lorena Wiebes powered to victory on stage one of Giro d'Italia Women, but was later kicked off the race for having an underweight bike, a finding that shocked her team.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Lorena Wiebes was kicked out of the race for riding an underweight bike.

item.title: Castro's indictment references a network of Cuban spies. Who were they?
item.description: In the indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro, prosecutors alluded to a network of spies called The Wasp Network. Here is what happened when they were deployed to Florida in the 1990s.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Steedman

media:group.media:description: Members of The Wasp Network became national heroes in Cuba after their capture.

item.title: Police urge community caution as man remains at large after Melbourne double stabbing
item.description: Victoria Police are urging people to be cautious after two stabbing incidents across Melbourne, including one where a car was driven into a home in the city's east.

media:group.media:description: Police allege a man drove a car into a house in Dandenong North and stabbed a female occupant.

item.title: US fires missile into ship trying to breach blockade to reach Iran
item.description: The US military fires a missile into the engine room of the merchant vessel trying to break through its blockade of Iranian ports, the Central Command says.

media:group.media:description: The Gambia-flagged cargo ship Lian Star is no longer transiting to Iran.

item.title: Lucky escape for pair as tree falls on car amid WA wild weather
item.description: Two people have had a lucky escape after a tree fell onto their car on a major highway in WA's South West this afternoon, as strong winds lashed the region.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Hanlon and Madigan Landry

media:group.media:description: The car was towed away after the incident.

item.title: Gaming an avenue for remote social connections outside sport
item.description: While sport is the lifeblood of some country towns, alternatives abound for those looking to build connections elsewhere.

media:group.media:description: Zac Cederholm runs a weekly boardgame night in Kununurra.

item.title: Love story: Locking down the 'one that got away'
item.description: For a long time, I considered my teenage crush the one that got away. That all changed when we reconnected two decades later.

media:group.media:description: Carlie Davies says reconnecting with Shannon Giles and having their daughter Rowan was the best thing ever.

item.title: Dozens of arrests during Champions League celebrations on Paris streets
item.description: Celebrations of Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory have turned violent, as fans clashed with police on the streets of the French capital.

media:group.media:description: Celebrations turned to chaos in Paris.

item.title: Trump to headline 250th anniversary fair after musicians cancel
item.description: Martina McBride, Bret Michaels, Young MC, Morris Day and The Time and The Commodores all said they would not perform after alleging they were misled about the nature of the event.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump said he was the number one attraction in the world.

item.title: Flotilla activists take evidence of alleged abuse to international court
item.description: Eleven Australians detained by Israeli forces while attempting to deliver aid to Gaza have submitted evidence to the International Criminal Court alleging abuse, torture and other violations during their detention.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty

media:group.media:description: Australian activists Zack Schofield, Neve O'Connor and Isla Lamont.

item.title: Rising Sun the only light needed in new Australian Dance Theatre work
item.description: The Australian Dance Theatre's first Indigenous artistic director had some dark days after the Voice referendum, but he shines a light on reconciliation in a new outdoor performance set to the rhythm of the rising Sun.
item.dc:creator: Charles Rushforth

media:group.media:description: The new production by the Australian Dance Theatre's RISE being performed at Bundanon on the NSW South Coast.

item.title: Socceroos defeated by Mexico despite encouraging second-half showing
item.description: The Socceroos are beaten 1-0 by Mexico in their penultimate World Cup warm up match despite an improved and impressive second-half performance at the Rose Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: Mo Toure missed a golden chance to equalise just before half-time.
